title: fak — the agent kernel · explainer reveal
source: authored 2026-06-21 from the in-repo deterministic diagrams (visuals/_meta.json + the hand-authored cards 39/46/48); captions adversarially verified against the share-kit 'What Not To Say' honesty rules. Companion to hero-video (benchmarks); this one leads with P1, the boundary.

scenes.headline: fak — the agent kernel
scenes.subcaption: Agents are programs. Give them permissions, quarantine, and proof.
scenes.footer: every claim shot is a real in-repo diagram — nothing generated

scenes.headline: The tool call IS a system call
scenes.subcaption: Each kernel concept maps to a classic OS mechanism — and the verdict comes from evidence the model didn't author, not its narration.
scenes.footer: tool call ≈ int 0x80 · Tool Table ≈ syscall ABI · DOS ≈ seccomp / LSM / eBPF

scenes.headline: Context is protected memory
scenes.subcaption: A tool result doesn't enter context just because the call ran — DOS adjudicates the result at an admission gate.
scenes.footer: PASS → protected context · FAIL → quarantine, then compress / drop / page-out

scenes.headline: Shared-prefix KV: copy-on-write across fleet
scenes.subcaption: A fleet boots from one preamble — computed once, held copy-on-write, while each agent owns only its small divergent suffix.
scenes.footer: the HBM saving grows with fleet size · a boundary steward stops the prefix at the public edge

scenes.headline: One loop, many chips
scenes.subcaption: A 2-D scheduler routes {which agent} × {which tier}: tiny CPU for pre-flight, small GPU for the hot path, frontier for the hard steps.
scenes.footer: route each token to the cheapest tier that can do the step

scenes.headline: fak — the agent kernel
scenes.subcaption: Permissions first, fail-closed. Filters second. Bring a trace — the verdict replays.
